description_long: This is a weathered, cylindrical bottle with a long, narrow neck and a slightly wider base. The surface is mottled with greenish and brownish hues, giving it a corroded, aged appearance, as if it has been exposed to the elements for a long time. On one side, there is a rectangular patch with the word 'botella' faintly embossed, suggesting it may have once been labeled or branded. The bottle appears to be made of metal, likely iron or steel, as indicated by the rust-like discoloration and matte finish. The top is open, lacking any visible cap or closure, and the interior is not visible, implying it is empty or opaque. Its utilitarian form and distressed look make it suitable for urban environments, possibly as discarded litter or a reused container.
description: A weathered, greenish-brown metal bottle with a long neck, corroded surface, and an embossed 'botella' label on one side. The bottle has a matte, aged finish and no visible cap.
description_view_0: The bottle is seen from the side, showing its cylindrical body and long neck with a mottled, corroded green surface and no visible label.
description_view_1: This view reveals the embossed 'botella' label on a rectangular patch, highlighting the bottle's branding and weathered texture.
description_view_2: The back of the bottle is visible, showing a darker, more heavily corroded surface with no label or distinguishing marks.
description_view_3: Another side view with a lighter greenish surface and irregular patches of corrosion, no label visible.
attribute_label_or_branding: Embossed text 'botella' on a rectangular patch, located on one side of the bottle, slightly faded and blending with the corroded surface.
attribute_damage_or_deformation: Visible surface discoloration and corrosion, with patches of rust and wear; no major dents or cracks.
